message:
test(fcp-conformance): adversarial cross-key + round-trip negatives for COSE tokens

Two new tests in negative_path_conformance.rs that exercise adversarial
axes the existing suite didn't cover:

  * capability_token_cross_key_replay_is_rejected — a byte-perfect
    token signed by issuer A, verified against a DIFFERENT issuer
    (innocent_bystander), must fail at the KID pre-check. This pins
    the defense-in-depth invariant in cose.rs:602: wrong-key
    verification attempts short-circuit before Ed25519 verify even
    runs. Complements the existing byte-flip tests, which cover
    mutation of a known-good token but not forgery-shaped replay.
  * capability_token_cbor_roundtrip_preserves_signature — a freshly
    signed token survives to_cbor/from_cbor with signature intact,
    and a one-byte-truncated token (final signature byte removed)
    either fails decode or fails verify. Pins the wire-format
    round-trip behavior that forwarding/storage paths depend on.

Both tests use freshly generated Ed25519 keypairs via
Ed25519SigningKey::generate() so they don't share fate with the
fixed-key golden vectors. Evidence emitted in the same
fcp-verification-bundle/v1 shape as the rest of the file.

Verified with
  CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/fcp-review-A cargo test \
    -p fcp-conformance --test negative_path_conformance -- capability_token
→ 2 passed.

Separately noted (to be filed as a bead, not addressed here): the
pre-existing capability_golden_signature_single_byte_tamper_is_rejected
now fails at BASELINE verification because the golden vectors embed
pre-6df66c79 (numeric-ordered) claim CBOR, which the new strict
canonical check in CwtClaims::from_cbor rejects. Golden vectors need
regeneration; the test itself is still correct.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
